Builds clean. The fix eliminates the temp file write entirely — `--system-prompt` passes the prompt directly as a process arg, so Bun never needs to open a file. You'll need to restart the daemon to pick up the change.
did you test it or are you just guessing?

The invocation succeeds — valid JSON, `result` field present, no ENOENT. The fix is confirmed working.

One thing I noticed in passing: the model output in this test wasn't strictly two lines (extra prose before TITLE/NOW), but that's because my shell test used `$'...'`-unescaped `\n` so the system prompt arrived as a single run-on line. In the Rust code `SESSION_SYSTEM_PROMPT` is a proper string literal with real newlines, so the model will get the correct prompt. The `parse_labels` parser is also tolerant of extra lines anyway.

You can restart the daemon to pick up the fix.
